articles.title: Allegiance
articles.author: Celia Stoddard
articles.complete_text:
You have your beauty, desert,
Gray dune upon gray dune,
And space enough to think wide thoughts
Beneath the low red moon.

And there be those who love you,
And think your wastes more fine
Than green spires of tamarack
Or bronze shaft of pine,

Or blue lakes shining silver,
Or clean smell of wood.
And I could love you, deseret,
Save these are in my blood.
articles.notes: This is a complete poem by Celia Stoddard about the desert/Utah, clearly literary content that should be standalone

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of advertisements for various products and services (paint, insurance, mattresses, garments, appliances, etc.), subscription and publication information for the Relief Society Magazine, a brief news item about an 83-year-old man returning to university, and a form for readers to provide names/addresses of friends in California. All legitimate miscellaneous content appropriate for this classification.
